perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees it shall apply a reasonable level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the same level' to 'a reasonable level' in Section 2 introduces ambiguity. The term 'reasonable' is subjective and open to interpretation. If a party treats the other's information with a lower level of security than its own, it may claim it was still 'reasonable,' even if a data breach occurs. This contradicts the intent of the NDA and could lead to legal disputes about what constitutes a 'reasonable level' of protection. This could contradict section 8 in the event of any violation, affecting its enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereby acknowledge that disclosure of any Confidential Information in violation of the terms hereof would cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party. Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available, each party is entitled to equitable relief through injunction if the other party or any of its employees, agents or representatives breaches any provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'the same level' of confidential treatment, which is a clear and objective standard. The changed text replaces this with 'a reasonable level,' introducing a subjective and ambiguous standard. This ambiguity directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'disclosure of any Confidential Information in violation of the terms hereof would cause irreparable harm.' The original text's objective standard would allow for easier enforcement and determination of a violation. The changed text's subjective standard makes it more difficult to determine what constitutes a violation, thereby weakening the contradicted text's enforcement and potentially causing irreparable harm as stated.

perturbation.original_text: The party receiving the Confidential Information (the "Receiving Party") may also disclose --------------- Confidential Information to a third party pursuant to paragraph 4(g) below and subject to the notice provision set forth therein.
perturbation.changed_text: The party receiving the Confidential Information (the "Receiving Party") may also disclose --------------- Confidential Information to a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference 'pursuant to paragraph 4(g) below and subject to the notice provision set forth therein' in section 3, this enables the receiving party to disclose confidential information to third parties without restrictions. Thus, if the party discloses confidential information to any party without notification, section 4(g) will directly contradict section 3, due to the missing clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations under this Agreement shall not apply to information that is at any time: (a) already known to the Receiving Party at the time it is disclosed to the Receiving Party; (b) publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully received from a third party without restriction on disclosure and without breach of this Agreement; (d) independently developed by the Receiving Party; (e) approved for release by <PAGE> written authorization of the party disclosing the Confidential Information (the "Disclosing Party"); (f) furnished by the Disclosing Party to a third party ---------------- without written restriction on disclosure; or (g) disclosed pursuant to a requirement of a governmental agency or of law, provided, however, that, to the -------- ------- extent viable under the circumstances, the party subject to the disclosure requirement has notified the Disclosing Party in advance of such disclosure and the Disclosing Party has had an opportunity to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and the party subject to the disclosure requirement has reasonably cooperated with such efforts; and provided further, however, that the -------- ------- ------- Receiving Party furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be so disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 3 allowed disclosure of confidential information to a third party only 'pursuant to paragraph 4(g) below and subject to the notice provision set forth therein.' The changed text removes this restriction, allowing disclosure without any notification as outlined in section 4(g). Section 4(g) specifies conditions for disclosure pursuant to a governmental agency or law, including prior notification to the Disclosing Party. The modified Section 3 contradicts Section 4(g) because it permits unrestricted disclosure, conflicting with the notification requirement specified in 4(g).

perturbation.original_text: If any party provides any of the other parties with written notification of its request for return of its Confidential Information, then the other party(ies) shall promptly return (unless destroyed pursuant to the immediately following sentence) to the notifying party all materials and information comprising the notifying party's Confidential Information, including any and all copies, facsimiles and reproductions thereof, and any other material containing or reflecting any materials or information in the Confidential Information. All other documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's representatives based on the materials or information in the Confidential Information shall be destroyed and such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the notifying party.
perturbation.changed_text: If any party requests return of its Confidential Information, then the other party(ies) shall return to the notifying party all materials and information comprising the notifying party's Confidential Information. All other documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's representatives based on the materials or information in the Confidential Information shall be destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'written notification' and 'unless destroyed pursuant to the immediately following sentence' along with 'including any and all copies, facsimiles and reproductions thereof, and any other material containing or reflecting any materials or information in the Confidential Information' and lastly 'and such destruction shall be confirmed in writing to the notifying party.', the requirement of written notification is removed, and only return is required, also the text is now omitting the destruction confirmation, and the explicit declaration of destroying every single copy of the confidential information. This affects section 7 which guarantees the property of the disclosing party, where the text is no longer enforced as strictly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information and intellectual property produced based on the Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Subject only to the Receiving Party's limited use of the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above in this Agreement, the Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, and the Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property or other rights in or to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement of 'written notification', the explicit instruction for destroying all copies, and the confirmation of destruction, which weakens the protection of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that all right, title, and interest in the Confidential Information remains the sole property of the Disclosing Party, and that the Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property rights. By removing the strict requirements for returning and destroying the Confidential Information, the changed text introduces a contradiction with the explicit guarantee of the Disclosing Party's property rights as outlined in the Contradicted Text. The omission of confirming destruction in writing also makes it more difficult to enforce the Disclosing Party's rights.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent via registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, return receipt requested; by facsimile transmission, with confirmation of receipt; or by overnight courier service to the other party as follows: If to Kaplan: If to Company: Ross Hamachek Vince Pisano ----------------------------- Kaplan, Inc. Flagler Dr. #300-E ----------------------------- 888 Seventh Avenue, 23rd Fl. West Palm Beach, FL 33401 ----------------------------- New York, NY 10106 ----------------------------- Facsimile: 212-492-5860 Any party may by notice as set forth in this paragraph change the address to which further notices to it shall be mailed.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent to the other party as follows: If to Kaplan: If to Company: Ross Hamachek Vince Pisano ----------------------------- Kaplan, Inc. Quest Education Corp. ----------------------------- 888 Seventh Avenue, 23rd Fl. 1400 Hembree Road #100 ----------------------------- New York, NY 10106 Roswell, GA 30076 ----------------------------- Facsimile: 212-492-5860 Copy to: Copy to: Veronica Dillon, Esq. Morris Brown, Esq. ----------------------------- General Counsel Greenberg Traurig ----------------------------- Kaplan , Inc. 777 S. Flagler Dr. #300-E ----------------------------- 888 Seventh Avenue, 23rd Fl. West Palm Beach, FL 33401 ----------------------------- New York, NY 10106 ----------------------------- Facsimile: 212-492-5860 Any party may by notice as set forth in this paragraph change the address to which further notices to it shall be mailed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'via registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, return receipt requested; by facsimile transmission, with confirmation of receipt; or by overnight courier service' from Section 12, the text removes the means in which the communication must take place. This creates uncertainty about how notifications must be sent, and whether an email is sufficient, affecting all other sections of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent to the other party as follows: If to Kaplan: If to Company: Ross Hamachek Vince Pisano ----------------------------- Kaplan, Inc. Quest Education Corp. ----------------------------- 888 Seventh Avenue, 23rd Fl. 1400 Hembree Road #100 ----------------------------- New York, NY 10106 Roswell, GA 30076 ----------------------------- Facsimile: 212-492-5860 Copy to: Copy to: Veronica Dillon, Esq. Morris Brown, Esq. ----------------------------- General Counsel Greenberg Traurig ----------------------------- Kaplan , Inc. 777 S. Flagler Dr. #300-E ----------------------------- 888 Seventh Avenue, 23rd Fl. West Palm Beach, FL 33401 ----------------------------- New York, NY 10106 ----------------------------- Facsimile: 212-492-5860 Any party may by notice as set forth in this paragraph change the address to which further notices to it shall be mailed.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the methods for sending notices (registered mail, courier, etc.). The changed text removes these specifications, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means that it lacks the means in which communication must take place, creating uncertainty. This directly contradicts the need for clear and defined communication methods as outlined in the original agreement and explanation.
